---
title: '<span style = "display:none;">PrecisionGo</span>'
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: false
site: workflowr::wflow_site
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .dt-wrap { padding: 1.5rem 0.25rem; font-family: var(--font-sans); }
  .dt-card {
    background: var(--color-background-secondary);
    border: 0.5px solid var(--color-border-tertiary);
    border-radius: var(--border-radius-lg);
    padding: 1.75rem 2rem;
    opacity: 1;
    transition: opacity 0.25s ease;
  }
  .dt-card.fading { opacity: 0; }
  .dt-step-label { font-size: 11px; color: var(--color-text-tertiary); letter-spacing: 0.07em; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
  .dt-question { font-size: 17px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--color-text-primary); margin-bottom: 0.4rem; }
  .dt-sub { font-size: 13px; color: var(--color-text-secondary); line-height: 1.6; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }
  .dt-options { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 1rem; }
  .dt-btn {
    background: var(--color-background-primary);
    border: 0.5px solid var(--color-border-secondary);
    border-radius: var(--border-radius-md);
    padding: 10px 20px;
    font-size: 14px;
    color: var(--color-text-primary);
    cursor: pointer;
    transition: background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, color 0.15s;
  }
  .dt-btn:hover { background: #E1F5EE; border-color: #0F6E56; color: #085041; }
  .dt-divider { border: none; border-top: 0.5px solid var(--color-border-tertiary); margin: 1.25rem 0; }
  .dt-breadcrumb { display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 1.25rem; }
  .dt-crumb { font-size: 12px; padding: 3px 10px; background: var(--color-background-primary); border: 0.5px solid var(--color-border-tertiary); border-radius: var(--border-radius-md); color: var(--color-text-secondary); }
  .dt-crumb-sep { font-size: 12px; color: var(--color-text-tertiary); }
  .dt-back { background: none; border: none; font-size: 13px; color: var(--color-text-tertiary); cursor: pointer; padding: 0; transition: color 0.15s; }
  .dt-back:hover { color: var(--color-text-secondary); }
  .dt-result-label { font-size: 11px; color: #0F6E56; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.07em; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
  .dt-result-title { font-size: 24px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--color-text-primary); margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
  .dt-result-desc { font-size: 14px; color: var(--color-text-secondary); line-height: 1.7; margin-bottom: 1.25rem; }
  .dt-restart { background: none; border: 0.5px solid var(--color-border-secondary); border-radius: var(--border-radius-md); padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--color-text-secondary); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s; }
  .dt-restart:hover { background: var(--color-background-primary); }
</style>

<div class="dt-wrap">
  <div class="dt-card" id="dt-card">
    <div id="dt-inner"></div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
const tree = {
  question: "What type of data are you capturing?",
  sub: "Select the primary measurement modality for your experiment.",
  options: [
    {
      label: "Transcriptomics",
      next: {
        question: "What spatial resolution do you need?",
        sub: "Consider the biological question — do you need to resolve individual cells?",
        options: [
          {
            label: "Single-cell resolution",
            next: {
              question: "Whole transcriptome or targeted panel?",
              sub: "Targeted panels offer greater depth for known genes; whole transcriptome is hypothesis-free.",
              options: [
                { label: "Whole transcriptome", result: "VisiumHD" },
                { label: "Targeted panel",      result: "Xenium" }
              ]
            }
          },
          { label: "Spot-based resolution", result: "Visium" },
          { label: "User-defined ROI",      result: "GeoMx" }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      label: "Proteomics",
      next: {
        question: "How many protein targets do you need?",
        sub: "High-parameter panels (40+) require different instrumentation than standard mIF.",
        options: [
          { label: "High-parameter (40+ markers)", result: "COMET" },
          { label: "Low-parameter (<10 markers)",  result: "mIF" }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
};

const descriptions = {
  "VisiumHD": "Visium HD captures whole-transcriptome data at 2µm bin resolution, enabling near-single-cell spatial analysis across a large capture area. Best suited for discovery-driven experiments where cell-level resolution and transcriptome-wide coverage are both required.<br><br><strong>Is this compatible with multiomics?</strong><br>Slightly. The 10X protocol allows for single round multiplex immunofluorescent imaging.<br><br><a href='vishd.html'>See the analysis pipeline for details</a>",
  
  "Xenium":   "Xenium provides single-cell resolution spatial transcriptomics using targeted in situ sequencing. Ideal when your gene panel is defined and you need high sensitivity, subcellular resolution, or large tissue coverage across multiple sections.<br><br><strong>Is this compatible with multiomics?</strong><br>Yes. Following the Xenium protocol, slides can be returned to the user and stained for proteomics. The caveat is that the 10X protocol may affect tissue integrity and/or the markers themselves.<br><br><a href='xen.html'>See the analysis pipeline for details</a>",
  
  "Visium":   "Standard Visium captures whole-transcriptome data at 55µm spot resolution. Each spot contains a mixture of cells, so deconvolution is required to infer cell type composition. Well-suited for large-area tissue profiling where single-cell resolution is not required.<br><br><strong>Is this compatible with multiomics?</strong><br>Slightly. The 10X protocol allows for single round multiplex immunofluorescent imaging.<br><br><a href='vis.html'>See the analysis pipeline for details</a>",
  
  "GeoMx":    "GeoMx DSP enables spatially resolved transcriptomics or proteomics from user-selected regions of interest. Ideal for hypothesis-driven experiments targeting specific tissue compartments identified by morphology or marker expression.<br><br><strong>Is this compatible with multiomics?</strong><br>Slightly. The GeoMx protocol allows for single round multiplex immunofluorescent imaging for contextual ROI selection.<br><br><a href='geomx.html'>See the analysis pipeline for details</a>",
  
  "COMET":    "COMET enables high-parameter spatial proteomics (40–100+ markers) with single-cell resolution. Suited for deep immune profiling and complex tissue microenvironment characterisation.",
  
  "mIF":      "Multiplex immunofluorescence supports low-parameter spatial proteomics (typically 4–8 markers) with high sensitivity and spatial resolution. Best for targeted validation, clinical samples, or when throughput and cost are priorities."
};

let path = [];
let current = tree;

function transition(renderFn) {
  const card = document.getElementById('dt-card');
  card.classList.add('fading');
  setTimeout(() => {
    renderFn();
    card.classList.remove('fading');
  }, 220);
}

function render() {
  const inner = document.getElementById('dt-inner');
  const bcHtml = path.length > 0
    ? `<div class="dt-breadcrumb">${path.map((p, i) =>
        (i < path.length - 1)
          ? `<span class="dt-crumb">${p.choice}</span><span class="dt-crumb-sep">›</span>`
          : `<span class="dt-crumb">${p.choice}</span>`
      ).join('')}</div>`
    : '';

  if (current.result) {
    const name = current.result;
    inner.innerHTML = `
      ${bcHtml}
      <div class="dt-divider" style="margin-top:0;"></div>
      <div class="dt-result-label">Recommended technology</div>
      <div class="dt-result-title">${name}</div>
      <div class="dt-result-desc">${descriptions[name]}</div>
      <button class="dt-restart" onclick="doRestart()">← Start over</button>
    `;
    return;
  }

  const stepNum = path.length + 1;
  inner.innerHTML = `
    ${bcHtml}
    ${path.length > 0 ? '<div class="dt-divider" style="margin-top:0;"></div>' : ''}
    <div class="dt-step-label">Step ${stepNum}</div>
    <div class="dt-question">${current.question}</div>
    <div class="dt-sub">${current.sub || ''}</div>
    <div class="dt-options">
      ${current.options.map((opt, i) =>
        `<button class="dt-btn" onclick="doChoose(${i})">${opt.label}</button>`
      ).join('')}
    </div>
    ${path.length > 0 ? `<button class="dt-back" onclick="doBack()">← Back</button>` : ''}
  `;
}

function doChoose(i) {
  const opt = current.options[i];
  path.push({ choice: opt.label, node: current });
  current = opt.next || { result: opt.result };
  transition(render);
}

function doBack() {
  const prev = path.pop();
  current = prev.node;
  transition(render);
}

function doRestart() {
  path = [];
  current = tree;
  transition(render);
}

render();
</script>
```

```
